sections.paragraph:
(contr. from גֶּנֶת, of the form בֶּנֶת, בַּת, for יְגֶנֶת from the root יָנַן), pl. גִּתֹּות f. [From נָנַז in Thes.]

(1) a wine-press, or rather the trough in which the grapes were trodden with the feet, whence the juice flowed into a vat (יֶקֶב) placed near, as it was squeezed from the grapes. Joel 4:13 . דָּרַךְ גַּת to tread a winepress, Neh 13:15 Lam 1:15.

(2) [Gath], pr. name of a city of the Philistines, where Goliath was born. Jos 13:3 1Sa 6:17 21:11 1Ki 2:39, 40 1Ki 2:40. Hence patron. גִּתִּי. [Gittite].

(3) גַּת חֵפֶר (“wine-press of the well”), [Gathhepher], a town of the tribe of Zebulon (with ה local, גִּתָּה חֵפֶר), Jos 19:13, celebrated as the birth-place of Jonah the prophet.

(4) גַּת רִמֹּון (“wine-press of the pomegranate”); [Gath-rimmon], a town of the tribe of Dan, Jos 19:45. [See Robinson, ii. 421].
